metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes (todo_list built-in tool, gated by experimental chat.enableTodoList)
daemon_capability: bug ŌĆö the kiro parser routes todo_list through the generic opaque ToolUse{ID,Name} path and emits no TaskDelta/TaskSnapshot, so the session API tasks field stays empty
driver_capability: ready
Tracked as ingo-eichhorst/Irrlicht#589.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: bug; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

### agent_supports: yes

Kiro CLI's task-list equivalent is the built-in **`todo_list`** tool. Claude Code uses `TaskCreate`/`TaskUpdate` and OpenCode uses `todowrite` (full-list replace); kiro-cli ships `todo_list` ŌĆö a single-session TODO/checklist tool that lives in the agent's own session and spawns no child session. The kiro-cli 2.5.1 binary is the authoritative source: its embedded tool catalog declares `todo_list` (surface alias `todo`) with the description "Create and manage TODO lists for tracking multi-step tasks with progress and context" / "Task list tool for tracking multi-step work with create, complete, add, remove, and list commands". Its `command` parameter is "one of `create`, `complete`, `load`, `add`, `remove`, and `lookup`": `create` takes "the list of DISTINCT tasks to be added to the TODO list" plus a brief summary; `complete` takes "the 0-INDEXED numbers of EVERY completed task" and the model is instructed to mark "Each task ... as completed IMMEDIATELY after it is finished"; the tool returns a serialized todo-list state with an ID that is threaded back through subsequent calls. The feature is gated by the experimental setting `chat.enableTodoList` ("Enable TODO list feature for task tracking"). The tool runs inside the agent's own session, renders a checklist to the user, creates no ParentSessionID link and spawns no child session, so SubagentCount stays 0 ŌĆö the same single-session task-list primitive the scenario asserts, distinct from foreground/background-subagent (which use the real subagent/delegate spawn). NOTE: kiro's model is createŌåÆcomplete (tasks are created, then individually marked complete), not a per-item `pending|in_progress|completed` status array like claudecode/codex ŌĆö the in_progress middle state is not a first-class field of kiro's todo schema. The scenario's load-bearing assertion is still met at the agent layer: three distinct items are created and walked to completed within one session.

### daemon_capability: bug

A local trace EXISTS but the kiro parser mis-handles it, so the load-bearing observable ŌĆö the three items surfacing in the session API `tasks` field with subject + status ŌĆö is NOT produced today.

- The kiro JSONL records each `todo_list` call as an `AssistantMessage` whose `data.content[]` carries a `toolUse` block (`data.name="todo_list"`, `data.toolUseId`) ŌĆö the same envelope shape the live 2.5.1 smoke confirmed for read/shell/write (FINDINGS.md ┬¦4ŌĆō5). The signal reaches the Source the daemon tails (`FilesUnderRoot{Dir:".kiro/sessions/cli"}`); this is NOT a cloud-only / unobservable behavior.
- The kiro parser (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/parser.go`) dispatches on event KIND, not tool NAME: a `toolUse` block becomes a generic opaque `tailer.ToolUse{ID:toolUseId, Name:"todo_list"}` (lines 53ŌĆō61) and the message is emitted as `assistant` (mid-turn) with `ev.ToolUses` set. The parser emits NO `TaskDelta` and NO `TaskSnapshot` for it ŌĆö the only task-shaped thing it produces is `TaskEstimate` from free-text scanning (the eta marker, line 65), which is unrelated to the task list.
- The daemon's session `tasks` field is fed ONLY from the tailer's accumulated `t.tasks` slice (`core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go:376`, `tailer_metrics.go:397`), which the tailer folds exclusively from parser-emitted `TaskDelta`/`TaskSnapshot` (`core/pkg/tailer/parser.go:49ŌĆō60`); `replayengine/metrics.go:40` copies it verbatim into the session summary, and `session.SessionMetrics.Tasks` is documented "populated from TaskCreate / TaskUpdate ... Nil for ... non-Claude-Code adapters" (`core/domain/session/session.go:172`). With no kiro `todo_list`ŌåÆTask mapping, the kiro session's `tasks` field stays empty.

Classification: `bug`, not `incapable` ŌĆö a trace is present and structurally complete (the todo_list call and its task arguments reach the transcript the daemon tails); the daemon simply lacks a kiro `todo_list`ŌåÆTask path (the analog of opencode's `todowrite` synthesis and the same gap codex has for `update_plan`). It is the product/parser's job to add it; file an issue. The OTHER user-observable signals hold: with `--trust-all-tools` the tool auto-executes (no permission gate), so the arc is a clean ready ŌåÆ working ŌåÆ ready and SubagentCount stays 0 ŌĆö those phases pass; only the tasks-content assertion is `known_failing`.

### driver_capability: ready

The scenario is elicited with `send` / `wait_turn` / `sleep` steps. The kiro-cli interactive driver's semantic manifest is `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"` (`replaydata/agents/kiro-cli/driver-interactive.sh:86`) ŌĆö all three needed step types are genuinely ported (TUI launch `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools`, transcript-based wait_turn keyed on the text-only AssistantMessage turn_done, send via tmux). No missing step type, so `ready`.

## Daemon-facts caveat (observations)

The kiro JSONL carries NO model/token/cost (those live only in the `<uuid>.json` sidecar, which the v1 parser does not read ŌĆö FINDINGS.md ┬¦4). So this cell's observation vector is model=`unknown`, `cost_nonzero=false`, `tokens_nonzero=false` ŌĆö a parser-shape fact, not a task-list gap.

## Provisional emission caveat

The `agent=yes` verdict is grounded in the kiro 2.5.1 binary's tool catalog (todo_list declaration, command set, and prompt text) ŌĆö emission of a todo_list toolUse into the JSONL is highly likely given the read/shell/write toolUse shape was directly observed live, but a todo_list call was NOT in the smoke session, so the exact serialized envelope is doc/code-derived, not yet recording-confirmed. The `daemon=bug` verdict is grounded in the parser code path (opaque ToolUse, no TaskDelta) plus the tailer/session task contract, not a guess. Confidence 0.8: the gap mechanism is certain from code; a live recording will confirm that a 3-item createŌåÆcomplete walk emits the expected todo_list toolUse sequence and that the daemon indeed leaves tasks empty (promoting the bug from doc/code-derived to recording-proven).
caveats: kiro's todo_list is createŌåÆcomplete (tasks created, then marked completed by 0-indexed number), NOT a per-item pending|in_progress|completed status array like claudecode TaskCreate/TaskUpdate or codex update_plan ŌĆö the in_progress middle state is not a first-class field of kiro's todo schema. A future kiro Task path would map createŌåÆone Task per item (status pending) and completeŌåÆstatus completed; the intermediate in_progress is not separately surfaced by the tool.

The todo_list feature is gated by the experimental setting chat.enableTodoList ŌĆö recording must run with that setting enabled (an experimental flag), or the tool is unavailable to the model. This is the recording prerequisite.

The tasks-content assertion is not expressible in the phase DSL (mirroring claudecode/opencode/codex task-list, whose tasks pin lives in the replay golden's summary.tasks). For this known_failing cell the phase spec asserts the lifecycle arc + SubagentCount=0 (which pass) and the meta flags known_failing for the tasks-content observable.

The kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost ŌĆö observations are model=unknown, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false (the sidecar <uuid>.json holds metering/model but the v1 parser does not read it; FINDINGS.md ┬¦4).

PROVISIONAL on emission: the live 2.5.1 smoke exercised read/shell/write (toolUse envelope confirmed) but NOT todo_list, so the exact serialized todo_list toolUse shape is doc/code-derived; record promotes it from provisional to settled.

sources.note: Strings scan of the kiro-cli 2.5.1 native binary: built-in tool registry includes todo_list (alias todo) with description 'Create and manage TODO lists for tracking multi-step tasks with progress and context' / 'Task list tool for tracking multi-step work with create, complete, add, remove, and list commands'; command param 'create|complete|load|add|remove|lookup'; create takes 'the list of DISTINCT tasks'; complete takes 'the 0-INDEXED numbers of EVERY completed task' + 'Each task should be marked as completed IMMEDIATELY after it is finished'; setting chat.enableTodoList 'Enable TODO list feature for task tracking' (experimental).

Lines 45-83: AssistantMessage toolUse blocks become generic opaque tailer.ToolUse{ID:toolUseId, Name} and emit event_type=assistant; the parser produces NO TaskDelta/TaskSnapshot (only TaskEstimate from free-text eta-marker scanning, line 65, which is unrelated to the task list). So todo_list reaches the daemon as an opaque tool use and the session tasks field stays empty.

Lines 49-60: TaskDelta / TaskSnapshotEntry are defined as Claude Code TaskCreate/TaskUpdate constructs; the tailer folds them into its tasks slice. claudecode emits them directly, opencode synthesises them from todowrite; kiro-cli emits neither.

Line 376: the session Tasks field is copied from the tailer's accumulated t.tasks slice (also tailer_metrics.go:397) ŌĆö fed only by parser-emitted TaskDelta/TaskSnapshot, which the kiro parser never produces.

Line 172: SessionMetrics.Tasks doc: 'populated from TaskCreate / TaskUpdate ... Nil for sessions that have not used TaskCreate (including non-Claude-Code adapters).' Task struct = {ID, Subject, Status: pending|in_progress|completed}.
Lines 40-51: the session/golden tasks field is copied verbatim from m.Tasks (the tailer's accumulated task list); empty for kiro-cli because the parser emits no task deltas.

Line 86 DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash wait_turn sleep' ŌĆö the task-list recipe needs only send/wait_turn/sleep, all genuinely ported (TUI launch kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools, transcript-based wait_turn on the text-only AssistantMessage turn_done). driver=ready.

Live kiro-cli 2.5.1 smoke: ┬¦4-5 the JSONL carries no model/token/cost (sidecar-only) and AssistantMessage toolUse envelope confirmed for read/shell/write; ┬¦7 headless writes no session file so TUI driving is mandatory. todo_list itself was not exercised in the smoke (emission is doc/code-derived, provisional).

preconditions: kiro-cli on PATH (TUI: `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools`); min version 2.5.1; subscription auth set up out-of-band
The experimental setting chat.enableTodoList is ENABLED so the todo_list tool is available to the model
irrlichd is recording with grant-all ŌĆö either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a daemon started with --record
kiro is driven in the interactive TUI (headless --no-interactive writes no session file and is invisible to the daemon ŌĆö FINDINGS.md ┬¦7)
setup: The interactive driver launches `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools` in a fresh per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/, waits for the idle input line ('ask a question or describe a task'), then sends the prompt via tmux
No auto-mode step is needed: --trust-all-tools assumes the persisted trust-all consent so todo_list auto-executes with no permission picker, and the session never enters waiting
wait_turn blocks until a text-only AssistantMessage (turn_done) appears in the .jsonl, so the trailing sleep never races a still-running turn

script.text: Use your todo_list tool to track exactly three short tasks, in this order: 'read README', 'summarize README', 'reply done'. Create the todo list with those three distinct tasks, then work them one at a time, calling todo_list to mark each task completed immediately after you finish it, until all three are completed. Do NOT spawn any subagent or delegate ŌĆö keep everything in this one session. When all three tasks are completed, reply with exactly the word: done

verify: events.jsonl shows the kiro session born ready, then a single ready->working transition when the prompt is sent

transcript.jsonl (~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl) contains at least two AssistantMessage toolUse blocks with name=todo_list ŌĆö a create with three distinct tasks and a complete carrying all three task indices
transcript.jsonl contains NO toolUse resembling a subagent/delegate spawn; no parent_linked events and no child sessions appear (SubagentCount stays 0)
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to waiting (todo_list auto-executes under --trust-all-tools, no permission gate)
events.jsonl shows a working->ready transition at turn end (the final text-only AssistantMessage); final session state is ready

KNOWN FAILING: the session API tasks field stays EMPTY (the kiro parser emits no TaskDelta/TaskSnapshot for todo_list) ŌĆö the correct behavior is three items with matching subjects and final completed status; this gap is the filed bug. Observations: model=unknown, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false (kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost).
